---
title: "data modeling by Yaxing Li"
author: "Yaxing Li"
date: "2025-03-07"
output: html_document
---

# Modeling

## 1.Preparation of data and packages
```{R}
library(rBayesianOptimization)
library(ParBayesianOptimization)
library(torch)    # PyTorch for R
library(torchvision)  # Data transformations (if needed)
library(keras)    # Keras for deep learning
library(tensorflow) # TensorFlow backend
library(tidymodels)  # Preprocessing and workflows
library("tidyverse")
library("tidymodels")
library("reticulate")
sessionInfo()
data <- read_csv("final_engineered_data.csv")
```

## 2.transforming the label variable
```{R}
data |>  dim()
data |> head()
# Split the data into training set
train_data <- data %>% 
  filter(split == "train") %>% 
  mutate(label=factor(label, levels=c('baseline','meditation','stress','break')))  # Convert the label column to factor

test_data <- data %>% 
  filter(split == "test")

```

## 3. check participants intersect
```{R}
train_subjects <- train_data%>% select(participant_id) %>% distinct()
test_subjects <- test_data %>% select(participant_id) %>% distinct()
intersect(train_subjects, test_subjects)
```

## 4.Train-Test split
```{r}
set.seed(123) 
# Remove 'split' from the dataset before creating the recipe
train_data_clean <- train_data %>% select(-split)
# Split the data
data_split <- group_initial_split(train_data_clean, prop = 5/6, group = participant_id) 
train_split_data <- training(data_split)
val_split_data <- testing(data_split)
```

## 5. Create the workflow
```{R}
train_recipe <- recipe(label ~ ., data = train_split_data) %>%
  update_role(chunk_id, new_role = "ID") %>%  
  update_role(participant_id, new_role = "ID") %>%
  step_naomit(all_predictors()) %>%             
  step_mutate_at(all_numeric_predictors(), fn = ~replace(., is.infinite(.), NA)) %>%
  step_impute_knn(all_numeric_predictors(), neighbors = 20) %>%
  step_normalize(all_numeric_predictors()) %>%
  step_dummy(all_nominal_predictors()) %>%
  step_corr(all_numeric_predictors(), threshold = 0.8) %>%
  step_zv(all_predictors())

# Prepare the recipe
prepped_recipe <- prep(train_recipe, training = train_split_data)

# Apply transformations
train_processed <- bake(prepped_recipe, new_data = train_split_data)
val_processed <- bake(prepped_recipe, new_data = val_split_data)

# Convert labels to numeric factors (for deep learning)
train_processed$label <- as.numeric(as.factor(train_processed$label)) - 1
val_processed$label <- as.numeric(as.factor(val_processed$label)) - 1

# Separate predictors and targets
X_train <- train_processed %>% select(-chunk_id, -participant_id, -label)
y_train <- train_processed$label

X_val <- val_processed %>% select(-chunk_id, -participant_id, -label)
y_val <- val_processed$label

# Convert to matrix format
X_train_matrix <- as.matrix(X_train)
X_val_matrix <- as.matrix(X_val)
```

## 6.Build a Deep Learning Model with Keras
```{R}

build_model <- function(lr = 0.001, dropout_rate = 0.3, l2_rate = 0.001, units_1 = 128, units_2 = 64) {
  model <- keras_model_sequential() %>%
    layer_dense(units = units_1, activation = "relu", kernel_regularizer = regularizer_l2(l2_rate)) %>%
    layer_batch_normalization() %>%
    layer_dropout(rate = dropout_rate) %>%
    layer_dense(units = units_2, activation = "relu", kernel_regularizer = regularizer_l2(l2_rate)) %>%
    layer_batch_normalization() %>%
    layer_dropout(rate = dropout_rate) %>%
    layer_dense(units = 4, activation = "softmax")  # 4 categories in the output layer
  
  model %>% compile(
    loss = "sparse_categorical_crossentropy",
    optimizer = optimizer_adam(learning_rate = lr),  # Updated to use learning_rate
    metrics = c("accuracy")
  )
  
  return(model)
}




```


## 7.Tune the hyperparameters
```{R}
objective_function <- function(lr, dropout_rate, l2_rate, units_1, units_2) {
  
  # Build the model
  model <- build_model(lr = lr, dropout_rate = dropout_rate, l2_rate = l2_rate, units_1 = units_1, units_2 = units_2)
  
  # Train the model
  history <- model %>% fit(
    X_train_matrix, y_train,
    epochs = 30,
    batch_size = 32,
    validation_data = list(X_val_matrix, y_val),
    verbose = 1,
    callbacks = list(callback_early_stopping(monitor = "val_loss", patience = 5))
  )
  
  # Get the best validation accuracy
  val_accuracy <- max(history$metrics$val_accuracy)  # Get best validation accuracy
  return(list(Score = val_accuracy, Pred = history))
}

# Define the hyperparameter search space, including l2_rate
bounds <- list(
  lr = c(0.0001, 0.01),  # Learning rate
  dropout_rate = c(0.2, 0.5),  # Dropout rate
  units_1 = c(64, 256),  # Units in the first dense layer
  units_2 = c(32, 128),  # Units in the second dense layer
  l2_rate = c(0.0001, 0.01)  # l2 regularization rate
)

# Run Bayesian optimization
bayes_opt <- BayesianOptimization(
  FUN = objective_function,
  bounds = bounds,
  init_points = 5,  # Number of random initial points
  n_iter = 25,      # Number of optimization iterations
  acq = "ei",       # Acquisition function (Expected Improvement)
  kappa = 2.5,      # Exploration parameter
  eps = 0.0         # Tolerance level
)

# View the results of the optimization
print(bayes_opt$Best_Par)
best_params <- bayes_opt$Best_Par


```

## 8.Train the final model with the best parameters
```{R}
# Extract and convert Best Parameters to numeric
lr <- as.numeric(best_params["lr"])
dropout_rate <- as.numeric(best_params["dropout_rate"])
l2_rate <- as.numeric(best_params["l2_rate"])  # Extract l2_rate from best_params
units_1 <- as.numeric(best_params["units_1"])
units_2 <- as.numeric(best_params["units_2"])

# Build Final Model
final_model <- build_model(lr, dropout_rate, l2_rate, units_1, units_2)

# Train Final Model
final_history <- final_model %>% fit(
  X_train_matrix, y_train,
  epochs = 30,
  batch_size = 32,
  validation_data = list(X_val_matrix, y_val),
  verbose = 1
)

```

## Make Predictions and Evalute the Model
```{R}
# Evaluate the final model
final_results <- predict(final_model, X_val_matrix)

# Make predictions on the test data
predictions <- final_model %>% predict(X_val_matrix)

# Convert predictions to class labels
pred_class <- apply(predictions, 1, which.max) - 1  # Subtract 1 because labels start from 0

# Convert numeric predictions back to factor labels
pred_labels <- factor(pred_class, levels = c(0, 1, 2, 3), labels = c('baseline', 'meditation', 'stress', 'break'))

# Create a data frame with participant_id, chunk_id, and predicted labels
submission_data <- data.frame(
  participant_id = test_data$participant_id,
  chunk_id = test_data$chunk_id,
  label = pred_labels
)
table(submission_data$label)
# submission_data %>%
  write.csv('DSW25_CNN_model_with_val.csv', row.names = FALSE)
```
